items.text:
Subject: Can You Cover My Saturday Barista Shift?

Hi Priya,

I hope your week is going well. I'm writing to ask a big favor. I'm scheduled to work the morning barista shift at the Maplewood Cafe this Saturday, October 11, from 7:00 AM to 12:00 PM, but my sister's graduation is that same morning in another city. Would you be willing to take my shift?

If you can cover it, we'll both need to fill out a Shift Swap Form and drop it at the Student Employment Office in Harmon Hall by Thursday at 5:00 PM. Forms turned in after that deadline carry a $10 late-processing fee, and the person requesting the swap is the one who pays it. I'd cover that charge if it came to it, though I'd much rather we get the form in on time.

I know this is short notice, and I'd gladly return the favor for one of your weekend shifts next month. Just let me know by tomorrow so I can plan around the day either way.

Thanks so much,
Devon

format_metadata.subject: Can You Cover My Saturday Barista Shift?

questions.stem: According to the email, what must Devon and Priya do to complete the shift swap?

options.A: Pay a $10 fee to the cafe manager
options.B: Submit a completed Shift Swap Form to the office
options.C: Register the change through the Maplewood Cafe portal
options.D: Contact the graduation office to confirm the date

questions.explanation: The email says the two of them need to fill out a Shift Swap Form and drop it at the Student Employment Office, which option B restates. Option A borrows the '$10' figure but misassigns it: that charge is a late-processing fee, not a payment to a manager. Option C invents a 'portal' the email never mentions. Option D reuses 'graduation' from the passage but attaches it to an office and an action that do not exist in the text.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the $10 late-processing fee?

options.A: Devon would prefer to submit the form before the deadline
options.B: Devon expects Priya to share the cost of the fee
options.C: Devon has already paid the fee to the office
options.D: Devon believes the fee will be waived for graduations

questions.explanation: Devon writes that he would rather they get the form in on time, implying he wants to avoid the fee by beating the Thursday deadline, so option A is supported. Option B reverses the stated logic: Devon says the requester pays and that he would cover the charge himself, not split it with Priya. Option C is unsupported, since no form has yet been submitted. Option D fabricates a waiver the email never suggests.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this email?

options.A: To report a scheduling error at the Maplewood Cafe
options.B: To explain the rules for submitting a Shift Swap Form
options.C: To request that a coworker take over a scheduled shift
options.D: To apologize for missing a shift without prior notice

questions.explanation: The whole email is built around Devon asking Priya to take his Saturday barista shift, which option C captures. Option A is wrong because nothing describes an error in the schedule. Option B names a real detail (the form rules) but treats a supporting step as the overall purpose. Option D misreads the tone: Devon is arranging coverage in advance, not apologizing for an absence.

items.text:
Notice: Scheduled Elevator Maintenance — Kingsley Residence Hall

Residents are advised that both passenger elevators in Kingsley Residence Hall will be taken out of service for annual inspection and modernization. The work has been scheduled by Facilities Management to reduce disruption during the academic term.

- Elevator A will be serviced from Monday, November 3 through Wednesday, November 5.
- Elevator B will be serviced from Thursday, November 6 through Friday, November 7.
- Daily work will be carried out between 8:00 AM and 6:00 PM.

During these periods, the stairwells at the north and south ends of the building must be used. Residents on the upper floors who need help moving heavy belongings may reserve a supervised freight cart for a refundable $20 deposit at the front desk in the lobby.

Please note that no work will be performed over the weekend, and both elevators are expected to resume normal operation by Monday, November 10. Questions may be directed to the Housing Office at housing@university.edu or 555-0247.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?

options.A: To announce new safety rules for using the stairwells
options.B: To collect deposits for the use of freight carts
options.C: To inform residents of a temporary elevator shutdown for maintenance
options.D: To request that residents avoid moving heavy items

questions.explanation: The notice exists to tell residents that both elevators will be out of service for scheduled inspection and modernization, which option C summarizes. Option A treats the stairwell instruction, a single supporting detail, as the main point. Option B does the same with the deposit rule. Option D misstates the freight-cart information as a request to avoid moving items, when the notice actually offers a way to move them.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what is required to reserve a supervised freight cart?

options.A: Signing a liability form at the Housing Office
options.B: Leaving a refundable deposit at the front desk
options.C: Reserving the cart before the weekend maintenance ends
options.D: Paying a nonrefundable fee to Facilities Management

questions.explanation: The notice states that the freight cart may be reserved for a refundable $20 deposit at the front desk, which option B restates. Option A invents a liability form and relocates the transaction to the Housing Office. Option C reuses 'weekend' and 'maintenance' but adds a timing condition the notice never sets. Option D borrows 'Facilities Management' and flips a key detail, calling the deposit a nonrefundable fee when the text says it is refundable.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about elevator service in the building during the maintenance week?

options.A: Both elevators will be out of service on the same days
options.B: Stairwell access will be blocked during the inspection
options.C: Freight carts will be provided in place of the elevators
options.D: One elevator will remain in operation throughout each weekday

questions.explanation: Elevator A is serviced Monday through Wednesday and Elevator B Thursday through Friday, so on any given weekday only one car is down and the other is running, supporting option D. Option A reverses this: the staggered dates ensure the two are never serviced together. Option B contradicts the text, which says the stairwells must be used, not that access is blocked. Option C misuses the freight cart, which assists with heavy items rather than replacing elevator service.
